channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: US domestic raw steel production week ending April 18, 2026
item.description: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,848,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 80.0%. Production was distributed across regions: Great Lakes (525k tons), Southern (823k tons), Midwest (295k tons), North East (133k tons), and Western (72k tons).

item.title: Scientists Develop Dirt-powered Fuel Cell That Could Replace Batteries
item.description: Scientists have developed a fuel cell that uses microbes in soil to produce electricity. The device can power underground sensors for tasks like monitoring moisture or detecting touch, without needing batteries or solar panels, and works in both dry and wet conditions.

item.title: Aluminum Price Falls to $3,530 Amid Supply Disruptions
item.description: Aluminum futures in the UK fell toward $3,530 per tonne, extending their retreat from a four-year high despite renewed concerns over supply disruptions from the Persian Gulf. Iran blocked the Strait of Hormuz again only hours after reopening it, blaming the US for its ongoing blockade of Iranian ports. The region accounts for roughly 9% of global output.

item.title: Zambia's copper smelters plan extended shutdowns
item.description: Copper smelters in Zambia, Africa's No.2 producer of the metal vital for clean energy, are planning extended shutdowns. This development could impact regional copper production capacity. The article is part of ongoing coverage on global mining news.

item.title: Global Aluminum Prices Consolidate at $3,555.13 Amid Historic Supply Shock
item.description: Global aluminum prices are consolidating at $3,555.13 per ton, up marginally 0.08%, as the market digests a historic supply shock anchored by extreme geopolitical disruptions. Elevated levels are driven by the ongoing maritime blockade in the Strait of Hormuz and targeted strikes crippling Middle Eastern smelting capacity at major EGA and Alba facilities. China's prices remain firm at ¥24,233 per ton, solidifying expectations of a prolonged global structural deficit.

item.title: U.S. Steel Price Outlook: HRC Climbs to $1,055, Production Hits Four-Year High, and Imports Down
item.description: HRC steel prices climbed to $1,055, with U.S. production hitting a four-year high and imports declining. The video provides outlook on these strengthening market trends. Published within the last 24 hours.

item.title: US raw steel production increases by 0.3 percent - week 16, 2026
item.description: US raw steel production saw a slight increase of 0.3 percent for week 16 of 2026. This update reflects recent domestic production trends amid ongoing industry monitoring. The report is part of SteelOrbis's latest steel news coverage.

item.title: India’s JSW Steel approves JV with POSCO to build 6 million mt steel mill in Odisha
item.description: JSW Steel has approved a joint venture with POSCO to construct a 6 million metric ton steel mill in Odisha, India. This development aims to expand production capacity in the region. The announcement highlights significant investment in new steelmaking infrastructure.

item.title: The Impact of Geopolitical Conflict on Ferrous Scrap and Steel Markets
item.description: Industry experts discuss how geopolitical tensions are impacting ferrous scrap availability, steel pricing, and logistics. The webinar highlights freight and logistics disruptions as major pressure points affecting the steel supply chain and procurement challenges.

item.title: Copper price movement - April 21, 2026
item.description: Copper fell to $6.01 USD/Lbs on April 21, 2026, down 0.39% from the previous day. Over the past month, copper prices have risen 10.53%, and are up 23.20% compared to the same period last year.

item.title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Steel prices fell to 3,129 CNY/T on April 21, 2026, down 0.29% from the previous day, though up 3.71% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures traded near a three-week high at around CNY 3,130 per ton due to firm seasonal demand and restocking. China Steel Corp. raised domestic prices for next month by NT$1,000 to NT$1,200 per ton, its fifth consecutive increase.

item.title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
item.description: Aluminum prices retreated 0.69% to $3,526.89 per ton globally and ¥24,064 in China, easing from recent four-year highs due to signals of easing supply pressures. Global prices consolidated at $3,557.12 per ton with a 0.14% decline amid geopolitical supply shocks from maritime blockades in the Strait of Hormuz and strikes on Middle Eastern smelters. Chinese prices remain firm at ¥24,244 per ton, supported by domestic demand and production caps, while depleted LME stocks and high energy costs influence sentiment.

item.title: Aluminum Holds Near 3-Year High
item.description: Aluminum futures in the UK fell toward $3,540 per tonne, retreating from a four-year high. China's output is expected to stall after hitting its 45 million ton cap in 2025, while smelters in Iceland, Mozambique, and Australia have suspended operations due to high energy costs and equipment failures.

item.title: Century Aluminum Begins Hot Metal Production at Mt Holly Plant
item.description: Century Aluminum has begun producing its first hot metal following the expansion of its Mt Holly plant in South Carolina, marking a significant capacity milestone.

item.title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
item.description: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers.

item.title: Copper hits one-month high as US-Iran talk hopes lift metal prices
item.description: Copper prices reached a one-month high driven by optimism from US-Iran talks. This positive sentiment lifted industrial metal prices amid broader market trends.

item.title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
item.description: Copper in concentrate production from Ivanhoe Mines’ Kamoa-Kakula complex fell to 61,906 tonnes in Q1, down 54% year-over-year, prompting evaluation of local third-party purchases to ramp up the smelter amid sulfuric acid shortages. Lundin Mining and BHP's Vicuña joint venture projects 395,000 tonnes annual copper production over 25 years. Strait of Hormuz closure threatens 40,000 t/month copper cathode flows to UAE as alternative ports near capacity.

item.title: Rising Copper Inventories & China's Smelter Surge Signal Near-Term Market Rebalancing
item.description: China, accounting for 60% of global refined copper demand, is expanding smelting capacity with monthly output nearing 1.2 million tonnes, contributing to rising inventories over 500,000 tonnes and short-term price correction. Despite near-term rebalancing, long-term supply constraints from 17-year mine development timelines and declining ore grades forecast a 30% global copper deficit by 2035.

item.title: Indonesian Nickel Processors Face Output Cuts Amid Benchmark Price Revision
item.description: Indonesian nickel processors are facing output cuts due to a benchmark price revision. This development is part of broader efforts to manage supply amid market pressures. The move could impact global nickel availability and prices.

item.title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News - Trading Economics
item.description: Aluminum rose to 3,629 USD/T on April 22, 2026, up 2.73% from the previous day, with futures in the UK moving past $3,600 per tonne near a four-year high. The continued blockage of the Strait of Hormuz threatens prolonged disruption to Middle Eastern supply. Recent signals of restored vessel flows through the strait have provided some relief, with prices dipping from the three-year high of $3,670 recorded on April 16th.

item.title: Goldman Sachs maintains 2026 copper price forecast and surplus projections
item.description: Goldman Sachs has maintained its average copper price forecast at $12,650 per tonne for 2026. The forecast reflects expectations for market surplus conditions in the coming months.

item.title: Italian HRC Index Rises 10% Since End-December; US Steelmaker Reports Stable Scrap Flows
item.description: The Italian HRC index rose 10% from end-December to €694.25/t ($812/t) ex-works. Steel Dynamics expects domestic ferrous scrap flows to remain stable over the next two quarters after weather-related supply tightness earlier in the year.

item.title: Copper price: Goldman maintains year-end forecast, Traxys sees $15,000
item.description: Goldman Sachs maintains its year-end copper price forecast amid ongoing market volatility. Traxys predicts prices could reach $15,000 per ton. The article discusses divergent analyst views on copper market trends.

item.title: SSAB secures €20 million funding for fossil-free steel R&D program
item.description: SSAB has secured €20 million in funding to advance its research and development program for fossil-free steel production. This initiative supports sustainable steelmaking technologies aimed at reducing carbon emissions. The funding underscores growing investment in green steel innovations.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today
item.description: Nickel futures are currently at 18,775.25 USD/T, up from the previous close of 18,690.75. The nickel prices outlook remains bearish as UBS warns of a persistent surplus through 2026 despite production cuts.

item.title: Nickel Spot Price Chart
item.description: Nickel spot price stands at 18,171.50, with a previous close of an unspecified value, 52-week low of 14,116.50, and high of 18,750.00. Historical prices are tracked up to April 24, 2026.

item.title: Price of Aluminum per Pound
item.description: Live aluminum price stood at 1.6524 USD per pound (bid) on April 24, 2026, down 0.08%. Kitco provides spot prices from New York, London, Hong Kong, and Sydney markets. The update reflects the latest trading session amid ongoing market volatility.

item.title: Copper fell to 5.98 USD/Lbs on April 24, 2026
item.description: Copper prices declined 1.57% to $5.98 per pound on April 24, 2026. Over the past month, copper has risen 8.06% and is up 23.55% year-to-date, showing overall strength despite the recent daily decline.

item.title: China's crude steel production up again in mid-April
item.description: China's steel production continued to increase at a slower pace in mid-April 2026.

item.title: FIMIGroup to Deliver Heavy-Gauge Leveling and Cut-to-Length Lines to JSW Steel
item.description: FIMIGroup announced plans to deliver two new heavy-gauge leveling and cut-to-length lines to JSW Steel in India. This equipment delivery represents technological advancement in steel processing capabilities.

item.title: China's Crude Steel Production Up Again in Mid-April
item.description: China's steel production continued to increase at a slower pace in mid-April 2026. This update reflects ongoing trends in the global steel market amid various economic factors.

item.title: US Midwest Domestic Hot‑Rolled Coil Steel Futures Prices Edge Higher
item.description: US Midwest domestic hot‑rolled coil steel futures have stabilized around 1,042–1,045 USD per ton in recent trading sessions, reflecting modest upward pressure in the domestic market. The data show relatively stable prices over the last few days, indicating a consolidating trend ahead of new demand signals.

item.title: ArcelorMittal Advances European Steel Sustainability with New Continuous Annealing Line in Belgium
item.description: ArcelorMittal has commissioned a new continuous annealing line in Belgium as part of its European steel sustainability initiatives. This advancement represents progress in modernizing steel production processes to reduce environmental impact.

item.title: Copper Futures Price Today
item.description: The current price of copper futures stands at 6.0270, down from the previous close of 6.0815. This reflects recent market movements in copper trading.

item.title: Air Liquide Invests Over $350 Million in Louisiana for Low-Carbon Steel Production
item.description: Air Liquide announced a major investment exceeding $350 million to strengthen its operations in Louisiana while supporting the growing demand for low-carbon steel production. This investment reflects industry momentum toward sustainable steelmaking technologies.

item.title: US steel production surges, poised to replace imports
item.description: US steel production is up 8.2% year-over-year with capacity utilization at 79.1%, positioning the industry to replace imports.

item.title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Copper rose to 6.04 USD/Lbs on April 27, 2026, marking a 0.28% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have risen 10.30%, and year-to-date gains stand at 24.44%. The market shows upward momentum amid recent stability near $5.5 per pound.

item.title: BHP now expects nearly 2M tonnes copper production after record Escondida throughput
item.description: BHP has revised its copper production expectations upward to nearly 2 million tonnes following record throughput at its Escondida mine. This represents a significant capacity achievement for one of the world's largest copper producers.

item.title: Live Aluminum Prices Today - Aluminum Price Analysis Today Apr 27, 2026
item.description: Global aluminum prices edged up 0.10% to $3,596.04/ton, with Chinese benchmarks at ¥24,535/ton. This reflects low LME inventories and the Strait of Hormuz blockade threatening 9% of global supply, though easing tensions and restored shipping cap rallies. China's production is constrained near 45-million-ton capacity amid sluggish demand before Labour Day.

item.title: Live Aluminum Prices Today - CarbonCredits.com
item.description: Global aluminum edged up 0.10% to $3,596.04/ton, with Chinese benchmarks at ¥24,535/ton. The marginal movement reflects tension between critically low LME inventories and the Strait of Hormuz blockade threatening 9% of global primary supply, though early signs of restored shipping and easing geopolitical tensions have capped further rallies.

item.title: Nornickel says nickel output flat year-on-year in first quarter
item.description: Russia’s Nornickel reported nickel output of 42,000 metric tons in Q1 2026, unchanged from the previous year. The company maintained its full-year forecast at 193,000-203,000 tons. This reflects steady production amid global market dynamics.

item.title: Nornickel keeps nickel output steady in Q1, cuts copper by 10%
item.description: Nornickel held nickel production at 42,000 tons in Q1 2026, matching last year, while copper output dropped 10% to 99,000 tons due to a higher base effect. The company cited operational factors in its statement. Nickel stability contrasts with copper declines.

item.title: Aluminum Price Analysis Today - April 27, 2026
item.description: Global aluminum edged up 0.10% to $3,596.04 per ton, with Chinese benchmarks at ¥24,535 per ton. The marginal movement reflects tension between critically low LME inventories and the Strait of Hormuz blockade threatening 9% of global primary supply, though early signs of restored shipping and easing geopolitical tensions have capped further rallies.

item.title: Metals On The Rise: Aluminium Up 12%, Nickel Hits 22-Month High, Copper Climbs
item.description: Aluminum prices have surged 12% this month amid rising metals markets. Nickel reached a 22-month high, while copper prices also climbed. The update highlights ongoing commodity price momentum in business news.

item.title: Live Nickel Prices Today
item.description: Nickel edged up 0.19% to $19,127.31/Ton globally and ¥130,526/Ton in China. Bullish momentum stems from Indonesia's slashed mining quotas tightening ore supply and a global sulfur shortage due to Middle East tensions impacting MHP processing. A weaker US dollar and firm SHFE quotes provide further support.

item.title: HRC Steel Reaches Highest Level Since January 2024
item.description: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel fell slightly to 1,105.97 USD/T on April 28, down 0.09% from the previous day, but has gained 4.99% over the past 4 weeks and reached its highest level since January 2024.

item.title: AISI: Raw steel production holds near multi-year high
item.description: Domestic raw steel production was estimated at 1,830,000 short tons in the week ending April 25, down 1.0% from the prior week but remaining near a multi-year high of 1,848,000 st set one week earlier. Production is 8.7% higher than the same week one year ago, with year-to-date production 6.0% higher than the same period in 2025. The mill capability utilization rate was 79.3% last week.

item.title: Nickel Enters a New Era as Indonesia Tightens Supply and Prices Surge
item.description: The global nickel market is shifting to a tightly managed pricing paradigm due to tightening supply and rising input costs, with prices surging to $19,200 per ton toward a $20,000-$21,000 target. Indonesia's calibrated strategy of mining quota reductions aims to stabilize prices in this range. The market has broken out of the $17,000-$18,000 range, now trading around $19,200.

item.title: Live Nickel Prices Today • Nickel Spot and Futures Price
item.description: Nickel prices advanced by 0.64%, settling at $19,364.63/Ton globally and ¥132,269/Ton in China, driven by supply constraints. Indonesia slashed mining quotas, tightening ore availability, while a global sulfur shortage from Middle East tensions threatens MHP processing. A weaker US dollar and firm SHFE quotes further support the bullish momentum for this battery metal.

item.title: Aluminium Up 12%, Nickel Hits 22-Month High, Copper Climbs
item.description: Aluminum prices surged 12% this month amid rising metals trends. Nickel reached a 22-month high, while copper prices also climbed. The update highlights ongoing momentum in commodity markets.

item.title: Steel Rebar Futures Rise to 3,150 CNY/T, Nearing Highest Level Since August 2025
item.description: Steel rebar futures rose to 3,150 CNY/T, supported by strong seasonal demand and restocking from end users. Chinese steelmakers raised domestic delivery prices reflecting higher input costs linked to the Middle East conflict. The World Steel Association projects global steel demand will increase 0.3% this year to 1.724 billion tons.

item.title: Copper fell to 5.87 USD/Lbs on April 29, 2026
item.description: Copper prices fell to 5.87 USD/Lbs on April 29, 2026, down 0.76% from the previous day, though up 7.24% over the past month and 28.38% over the year. Copper futures steadied around $5.95 per pound after four consecutive sessions of decline, supported by long-term fundamentals despite near-term pressures from the Iran conflict. Earlier, prices hovered around $6 per pound amid stalled US-Iran peace efforts and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: U.S. Steel to Build US$1.9 Billion DRI Facility at Big River Steel Works
item.description: United States Steel Corporation announced a US$1.9 billion investment to construct a new direct reduced iron (DRI) facility at Big River Steel Works in Osceola, Arkansas, marking the first DRI facility of its kind in the U.S.

item.title: Steel Prices Rise to 3,195 CNY/T on Strong Seasonal Demand
item.description: Steel prices in China rose to 3,195 CNY per ton, up 1.59% from the previous day, supported by strong seasonal demand and restocking by end users. Rebar futures have climbed above 3,150 CNY per ton, nearing their highest levels since August of the previous year, amid expectations of higher global steel demand.

item.title: Steel Market Update: Flat‑Rolled Steel Prices and Trends
item.description: Steel Market Update reports that flat‑rolled steel prices are firming on improving order books and tighter inventories, with lead times extending modestly. The publication highlights rising sentiment among service centers and mills as restocking activity picks up in key industrial sectors.

item.title: Steel Prices, News and Analysis from Global Transactions
item.description: SteelOrbis provides updated steel price assessments and market commentary, noting that global steel prices are underpinned by steady demand from construction and infrastructure projects. Regional price differentials remain elevated, reflecting logistics constraints and varying supply‑demand balances.

item.title: Steel Market Prices, Data, and Analysis
item.description: S&P Global reports that steel spot prices are trending higher across several regions, driven by restocking and expectations of improved demand in the second half of the year. The firm highlights that real‑time data and forward‑looking indicators point to a gradual tightening of the global steel market.

item.title: HRC Steel Price Rises to 1075.00 USD/T, Highest Since January 2024
item.description: Hot‑Rolled Coil (HRC) steel prices increased to 1,075.00 USD per ton, the highest level since January 2024, reflecting tightening supply and resilient demand. Over the past four weeks, HRC steel has gained about 4.99%, and analysts expect prices to rise further to around 1,120.96 USD/T by the end of the quarter.

item.title: Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
item.description: Chilean mining company Codelco announced that its El Teniente smelter is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact global copper supply dynamics.

item.title: Copper futures steadied around $5.9 per pound after five consecutive sessions of losses
item.description: Copper fell to 5.91 USD/Lbs on May 1, 2026, down 0.20% from the previous day. Industrial metals drew support from stronger-than-expected expansion in China's manufacturing activity despite ongoing pressure from the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Copper rebounds toward $13000 on supply squeeze
item.description: Copper prices have staged a sharp recovery to around $13,000 per metric ton after briefly falling below $12,000/mt, driven by mine disruptions and a renewed risk-on tone in global markets. Chile reported an 8.4% month-on-month drop in February copper output to 375,400 metric tons, while Peru's production declined 0.9% to 201,414 metric tons. The rebound highlights ongoing supply squeeze pressures in the copper market.

item.title: Free US Steel to invest $1.9 billion to build DRI facility at Big River Steel Works
item.description: US Steel is investing $1.9 billion to construct a Direct Reduced Iron (DRI) facility at its Big River Steel Works location. DRI technology represents a lower-emissions alternative to traditional blast furnace steelmaking methods.

item.title: US Manufacturing Growth Driven by Steel and Aluminum Price Increases
item.description: US manufacturing activity grew in April for the fourth consecutive month, with the ISM purchasing managers index at 52.7. The prices index surged to 84.6, the highest since April 2022, driven by increases in steel and aluminum prices, tariffs, and petroleum-based products due to Middle East conflict.

item.title: US Manufacturing Activity Grows for Fourth Consecutive Month
item.description: US manufacturing activity continued to expand in April for the fourth consecutive month, with order growth outpacing production. Steel prices were boosted by Middle East Gulf tensions.

item.title: Argentina's crude steel output increases 42.3 percent in March, amid erratic demand
item.description: Argentina's crude steel production surged by 42.3 percent in March despite facing erratic demand conditions. This notable increase highlights resilience in the local steel sector. The data reflects recent developments in Latin American steel output.

item.title: US Steel Production Reaches 80.4% Capacity Utilization
item.description: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,856,000 net tons in the week ending May 2, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 80.4 percent. This represents the highest capacity utilization level since August 2024.

item.title: Copper price analysis: Chinese demand offsets stalled US-Iran peace talks
item.description: Analysis of copper price movements driven by Chinese demand factors and geopolitical developments. The article examines how international relations impact copper market dynamics.

item.title: Alba Reduces Capacity: 3 Lines Shutdown Impact
item.description: Alba has shut down three reduction lines, reducing annual production capacity by approximately 308,000 tonnes from its total 1.62 million tonnes facility capacity, representing a strategic reduction to 81 percent operational capacity. This reduction represents 5.6 percent of total regional Middle Eastern output and approximately 0.51 percent of global aluminium production.

item.title: US to build largest aluminum plant with 500,000-ton annual capacity
item.description: The United States is preparing to establish its first aluminum smelter in over three decades with an annual production capacity of 500,000 tons, to be built by Century Aluminum in Inola, Oklahoma. The facility will double the country's primary aluminium production capacity and include 100,000 tons of high-purity aluminum designated for defense uses, with support from a $500 million OECD grant and partnership with Emirates Global Aluminum.

item.title: Pennsylvania Steelmaker Plans Environmental Upgrades
item.description: Re-roller Gautier Steel has been awarded a US$1.9 million grant for upgrades that will lower carbon emissions and improve energy efficiency. This initiative represents continued investment in sustainable steel production technologies.

item.title: US manufacturing grew in April
item.description: US manufacturing PMI held at 52.7 in April, marking four consecutive months of growth, driven by new orders rising to 54.1 while production eased slightly. The prices index surged to 84.6, the highest since April 2022, fueled by increases in steel and aluminum prices, tariffs, and petroleum products amid the Middle East conflict. Export orders contracted further, signaling challenges in global demand.

item.title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: HRC Steel fell to 1,120.05 USD/T on May 5, 2026, down 1.66% from the previous day, after reaching 1100.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, Hot-Rolled Coil Steel gained 3.79%. The price reflects recent volatility in the market.

item.title: Steel prices, news & market analysis
item.description: US manufacturing PMI held at 52.7 in April, with the prices index surging to 84.6 due to steel and aluminum price increases, tariffs, and Middle East conflict impacts. New orders rose while production eased slightly. Inflation risks have surged amid the war.

item.title: Greenland Resources signs long-term supply agreement with Cogne Acciai Speciali
item.description: Greenland Resources signed a long-term supply agreement with Italian specialty steel manufacturer Cogne Acciai Speciali for molybdenum and magnesium from its Malmberg project in east Greenland. The agreement supports Cogne's specialty steel production operations.

item.title: Aluminum rose to 3,575.75 USD/T on May 5, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices rose 1.63% to $3,575.75 per ton on May 5, 2026. UK aluminum futures rebounded to $3,530 per tonne from a three-week low of $3,480 on April 29th, driven by expectations of prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East.

item.title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
item.description: The United States is facing a serious industrial supply crisis with Bloomberg warning that American aluminum supplies could hit zero within 30 days. A 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum designed to protect domestic production has triggered a chain reaction threatening the entire American manufacturing base. Production disruptions across the Middle East have left American manufacturers with almost no major foreign sources to fall back on.

item.title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
item.description: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum. This move reflects the company's response to current market conditions and supply pressures.

item.title: Supply crunch drives aluminum to four year high
item.description: Stephen Hare, lead economist at Oxford Economics, discusses the outlook and current state of aluminum amid supply constraints. The supply crunch has driven aluminum prices to a four-year high.

item.title: Goodbye to critical metals? Scientists create a new revolutionary aluminum
item.description: Scientists have developed a new aluminum with revolutionary properties that could redefine production processes and significantly reduce dependence on critical metals, which are currently expensive and limited. This innovation could help slash industrial costs and accelerate the energy transition.

item.title: Ball Corporation reports record 2025 results as aluminium sustainability metrics advance
item.description: Ball Corporation published its 2025 Combined Annual and Sustainability Report, highlighting record-breaking financial results and significant progress on sustainability targets. The company achieved 74% recycled aluminum usage across its global beverage packaging business in 2025.

item.title: Green Aluminum: How Emerus is reducing its CO₂ footprint and building a sustainable future
item.description: Emerus focuses on reducing its CO₂ footprint through green aluminum production methods as part of building a sustainable future. The company emphasizes that sustainability is not a trend but a market necessity.

item.title: Steel Prices Rise to 3219 CNY/T, Supported by Strong Seasonal Demand
item.description: Steel rebar futures rose toward CNY 3,200 per ton, reaching their highest levels since August 2025, driven by strong seasonal demand and restocking from end users. Chinese steelmakers have raised domestic delivery prices reflecting higher input costs linked to the Middle East conflict. The World Steel Association projects global steel demand will increase 0.3% this year to 1.724 billion tons.

item.title: HRC Steel Falls to 1132.96 USD/T Amid Market Volatility
item.description: HRC Steel declined 0.44% to 1,132.96 USD/T on May 6, 2026. Despite the daily decline, HRC Steel prices have risen 5.00% over the past month and are up 27.30% year-over-year.

item.title: Sheet and Plate Prices Remain on Upward Trend
item.description: Sheet and plate prices continued their upward trajectory as industry sources increasingly questioned whether improved demand might be driving the market as much as limited supply. US steel shipments increased in March compared to both February and year-over-year levels.

item.title: SUMMSEED Project: Creating More Sustainable Steel with Lower Emissions
item.description: SUMMSEED is an international project focused on developing more sustainable steel with a lower greenhouse gas emissions footprint compared to steel manufactured using traditional techniques. The project represents innovation in reducing the environmental impact of steel production.

item.title: European Commission Unveils Action Plan to Safeguard EU Steel Production
item.description: The European Commission has announced a comprehensive action plan designed to protect EU steel production through import restrictions, enhancements to the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), and support for low-carbon steel technologies. This initiative aims to strengthen the competitiveness of European steelmakers while promoting sustainable production methods.

item.title: US Energy Department Pilot Project Measures Greenhouse Gas Intensity of Steel
item.description: The US Energy Department launched a pilot project to measure the greenhouse gas intensity of industrial products, including steel. This measurement system aims to help domestic steelmakers differentiate themselves from competitors and support efforts to tackle steel emissions.

item.title: Copper futures rose above $6 per pound on Wednesday
item.description: Copper futures advanced for a second straight session, rising above $6 per pound amid improving sentiment around a potential US-Iran agreement. The rally is supported by signs of improving demand in China and stronger-than-expected manufacturing expansion, despite ongoing headwinds from Middle East conflict.

item.title: Copper prices hit a new record high
item.description: Copper has hit a fresh record high, with Bloomberg Intelligence and Blue Line Futures analysts weighing in on whether the metals trade is 'stretched'. The analysis includes expert commentary on commodity market movements.

item.title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
item.description: Explores how new technologies and favorable policies can bridge the looming copper supply-demand gap as demand grows from 26 million tonnes in 2024 to 35 million tonnes by 2035. Highlights novel grinding technologies and innovations to exploit primary sulfide ores, improve mining efficiency, and reduce environmental impacts like water usage and habitat destruction.

item.title: Technology Minerals unlocks new innovation and revenue stream with copper and aluminium recovery
item.description: Highlights Technology Minerals' innovations in copper and aluminium recovery from batteries, creating new revenue streams and sustainable solutions. Focuses on circular economy approaches to copper supply challenges.

item.title: Nickel rose to 19913.88 USD/T on May 6, 2026, up 1.42% from the previous day
item.description: Nickel futures traded above $19,400, marking a near two-year high as supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability reinforced bullish momentum. Prices were underpinned by mining quota cuts in Indonesia and a sulfur shortage linked to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, which raised processing costs for nickel intermediates.

item.title: The current price of Nickel futures is 19,927.25
item.description: Nickel futures reached 19,927.25 USD with a previous close of 19,607.00, reflecting continued strength in the market. Despite UBS warnings of persistent surplus through 2026, recent production cuts and supply disruptions continue to support prices.

item.title: Indonesia nickel makers trim battery-feed output as sulphur squeeze bites
item.description: Indonesian nickel processors have trimmed output by at least 10% due to a sulphur shortage caused by disruptions from the Iran war. The cuts affect plants processing nickel ore into mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) for EV battery materials, with facilities backed by Chinese firms like Huayou Cobalt, Lygend Resources, and Tsingshan Group impacted. While overall nickel output remains high, MHP supply is tightening with low inventories at several firms.

item.title: Nickel rose to 19942.50 USD/T on May 6, 2026
item.description: Nickel futures traded above $19,400, marking a near two-year high driven by supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability. Mining quota cuts in Indonesia have constrained ore supply, while a sulfur shortage linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions has raised processing costs and tightened output conditions for downstream processors.

item.title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
item.description: Analysts expect nickel prices to reach $20,000 per ton within the next month as Indonesia commits to flat-to-down ore production targets of 250-260 million tons for 2026. EV demand remains robust with 20% underlying growth, while nickel consumption is projected to grow 6-7% annually, supported by completed destocking in the lithium supply chain.

item.title: Tenaris receives ninth consecutive worldsteel Sustainability Champions recognition
item.description: Tenaris has been recognized as a worldsteel Sustainability Champion for the ninth consecutive time, demonstrating its ongoing commitment to sustainable practices in steel production.

item.title: Tenaris announces $11 million USD digitalization and automation investment
item.description: Tenaris has announced a significant investment of $11 million USD in digitalization and automation initiatives to enhance operational efficiency.

item.title: Tenaris awards Roberto Rocca Scholarships to engineering students
item.description: Tenaris has awarded three Roberto Rocca Scholarships to engineering students from King Fahd University of Petroleum & Minerals in Saudi Arabia, reinforcing its commitment to developing local engineering talent in the Kingdom.

item.title: Tenaris publishes 2025 Sustainability Statement
item.description: Tenaris has published an executive summary of its 2025 Sustainability Statement included in its annual report, outlining how sustainability is embedded in its industrial strategy amid a complex geopolitical and market environment.
